TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Seediq, story: sdqCon-dialog1_ciwas_tiwas 2021s

S.FORM: o ini osa.
S.TRANSL: 是，（他）沒去。
He did not go there.

S.FORM: de bubu su de knduwa naq wada ka deheya na ini.
S.TRANSL: 而妳媽媽他們卻去了霧社，沒...
But your mother and some people went there. Not...

S.FORM: inu.
S.TRANSL: （去）哪裡？
You said they went where?

S.FORM: wada paran deheya.
S.TRANSL: 他們去霧社？
They went to Paran?

S.FORM: meniq moda mita yaku ka de temu mona han wa ini kii ka seediq.
S.TRANSL: 當Temu Mona來探望我的時候，那時還沒起義（殺日本人）。
When Temu Mona came to see me, they had not yet planned (to kill the Japanese).

S.FORM: ciida un un.
S.TRANSL: 那時.. 是的。
At that time ... You are right.

S.FORM: so kiya un ma so kusun na um so mgrebu mgrebu caman so kusun ka muda hii baso.
S.TRANSL: 是... 隔天，隔天的一大清早Baso經過那兒（你家）。
It was the next day when Baso passed your house in the early morning.

S.FORM: un so kusun o um a smnipaq hasuso alang mhebu hini wa hini sapah mu sipo hako nii di.
S.TRANSL: 到了第二天...殺了馬赫坡派出所(駐在所)（的日警）。我家在這裡，這兒是橋頭對面的一端。
On the second day, they killed the policemen at Mbehu. My house was here, just across the bridge.

S.FORM: un um alang mhebu wa hiya.
S.TRANSL: 馬赫坡部落在那邊。
Mhebu village was there.

S.FORM: um um um um um mlawa elu.
S.TRANSL: 是從（站在）路上對我說的。
He said to me when standing on the road,

S.FORM: X um um um iya osa paran a enaq hini kesaun ku na.
S.TRANSL: 他對我說「不要去霧社，留在這裡」。
"Do not go to Paran. Stay here."

S.FORM: um huN ini ku naq mkmusa yaku.
S.TRANSL: 我自己是不想去的。
I myself did not want to go there.

S.FORM: un ma de bubus bubu su ka wada.
S.TRANSL: 是你媽媽她們去了嗎？
Did your mother go there?

S.FORM: maha ka yaku kesi bubu mu.
S.TRANSL: 我媽媽說她要去。
My mother said she would go there.

S.FORM: kingan bruru napa.
S.TRANSL: 她揹回一簍（的衣物）。
She carried back one basket of clothes.

S.FORM: um lukus tahtunux.
S.TRANSL: 是日本人的衣物嗎？
Are they the Japanese's clothes?

S.FORM: lukus tahtunux wa.
S.TRANSL: 是日本人的衣物。
Yes, it was the Japanese's clothes.

S.FORM: um wada inu di lukus na.
S.TRANSL: 那些衣物不知到哪裡去了，是否...
I do not know where those clothes went.

S.FORM: adisun na mhebu hiya.
S.TRANSL: 她把衣服帶到馬赫坡部落。
She brought them to Mhebu Village.

S.FORM: ini ku na.
S.TRANSL: 沒（分給我）。
No, she didn't.

S.FORM: meyah naq mrinah paran han.
S.TRANSL: 來到霧社上空即折返（指日本偵察機）。
(The Japanese aircraft) came to Paran and then returned.

S.FORM: qitaan namu.
S.TRANSL: 妳們看到了嗎？
Did you all see it?

S.FORM: qitaan meyah baro han.
S.TRANSL: 看到了，因為（偵察機）飛到上面（空中）。
We saw it since they flew just above us in the sky.

S.FORM: o ma meyah ini dehuk mhebu.
S.TRANSL: 喔來到.. 沒抵達馬赫坡（上空）？
Did they come over to Mhebu?

S.FORM: ini eyah han ini eyah so ciida han.
S.TRANSL: 沒來到（馬赫坡上空），那時還沒來（到馬赫坡上空）。
No, they didn't. By that time, they had not come to Mhebu.

S.FORM: um um huN huN ma meniq su sapah ciida dung.
S.TRANSL: 是。是。那時妳還留在家裡嗎？
I see. At that moment did you still stay at home?

S.FORM: meniq ku sapah.
S.TRANSL: 我留在家裡。
Yes, I stayed home.

S.FORM: knduwa su so taqi hii ciida keeman ciida.
S.TRANSL: 那天晚上妳還是在自己家過夜嗎？
Did you sleep at home alone that night?

S.FORM: taqi ku hiya han um.
S.TRANSL: 我還是在那裡睡。
Yes, I still slept there.

S.FORM: anu so kusun du wa um kiya so knuwan meyah mhebu sapah skiya da pawan.
S.TRANSL: 到了第二天.. ，Pawan（蔡茂琳），飛機飛到馬赫坡上空是什麼時候？
When it was the following day... Pawan, when did the aircraft come to Mhebu?

S.FORM: kii da so kusun.
S.TRANSL: 就是第二天了。
It was exactly the following day.

S.FORM: meyah so kusun kika ma.
S.TRANSL: 隔天來了。
They came on the following day.

S.FORM: uxe na pawan.
S.TRANSL: 還不是那個時候哦 Pawan。
No, it wasn't at that time, Pawan.

S.FORM: kwana naq msseli hii kana neyah puku di.
S.TRANSL: Suku的族人怎麼可能會那麼快在那兒集結?
How come Suku people gathered there so quickly?

S.FORM: bakudang.
S.TRANSL: 炸彈。
A bomb attack.

S.FORM: um ye qnqda hikoki kii.
S.TRANSL: 嗯... 那可能是飛機投下的炸彈。
It seems that the aircraft dropped the bombs.

S.FORM: gisu tggarang kana a so supih.
S.TRANSL: 鍋子（炊具）到處散落（因炸彈爆炸所致）。
The pans were scatterd all over the place.

S.FORM: un mosa mesa mqiri.
S.TRANSL: 是的，因而去探頭看看。
That's right. We extended our heads out to see them.

S.FORM: ye na hilie mesa maanu na.
S.TRANSL: 族人們以為（飛機）是何物？
Did our people wonder what that was in the sky?

S.FORM: qitai na ma ini qduriq.
S.TRANSL: 他們沒逃開。
They did not run away.

S.FORM: seediq naq mhebu ciida.
S.TRANSL: 當時僅是馬赫坡部落的族人嗎？
At that time, were there only people of Mhebu?

S.FORM: tena suupu di kana.
S.TRANSL: 已聚集在一起了嗎？
Did you already gather there?

S.FORM: o kana ka kingan kumi mnkuni di peni um um um dehuk hiya kana seediq di.
S.TRANSL: 當時起義抗日的一組人馬(六個部落族人)都(在馬赫坡部落)聚集。
All the people (of the six villages who protested against Japanese) gathered there.

S.FORM: smalu a halung limuk halung kiya a um peni um musa rmengo kiya rmengo hikoki di na.
S.TRANSL: 製作槍彈... 那是槍枝要用的子彈... 去... 飛機...
We made guns and bullets. They are bullets for the guns. The airplane went...

S.FORM: wisu hokoki bino ma.
S.TRANSL: 當飛機飛過來時。
When the aircraft approached us,

S.FORM: ini kela mita hikoki na laqi kana rudan musa mesa mqiri peni mqiri winu uxe na angalun sasing ka so kiya di.
S.TRANSL: （族人們）可能因為沒看過飛機，即便是大人或小孩（都一樣），都（好奇地）去觀看飛機。那怎麼不被（飛機上的日本飛行員）發現呢?
Perhaps because we had never seen one, we, children or adults, all went to see the aircraft out of curiosity. How could we not get spotted by the Japanese aircrafts?

S.FORM: mosa cususuy mqiri uh bebe repun sdoi repun.
S.TRANSL: (族人們）大家都擠在穀倉的屋簷下觀看（飛機）扶著穀倉（觀看飛機）。
We all stayed under the eaves leaning against the storehouses, and watched these aircrafts.

S.FORM: X kndalax ciida cghuun da.
S.TRANSL: 從那時（族人們）即開始上吊自殺。
From that time, we people began to hang oneself

S.FORM: kndalax ciida da cghuun.
S.TRANSL: 從那時（族人們）開始上吊自殺。
From then on, our people began to hang themselves.

S.FORM: kndalax naq alang mhebu kiya sghuun di.
S.TRANSL: 從馬赫坡開始上吊自殺。
The self-hanging started from Mhebu.

S.FORM: ma qduriq namu.
S.TRANSL: 你們逃難。
You all escaped for lives.

S.FORM: um hum chuun naq shuun ma qduriq naq cakus ge qduriq naq cakus.
S.TRANSL: 什麼... 上吊的上吊，逃到Cakus的就逃到Cakus。
Some hung themselves, while others escaped to Cakus.

S.FORM: um ma peeyah buku bukuy alang daya a.
S.TRANSL: 從馬赫坡部落後上的Daya部落方來。
(The Japanese airplanes) came from behind Daya tribe.

S.FORM: wa hii kingan neepah daha kingan sapah o.
S.TRANSL: 那裡的耕地有間耕地住屋。
There was a house on the field.

S.FORM: sipan sipan hii na.
S.TRANSL: Sipan之地那兒。
That was in Sipan's place.

S.FORM: hbaro seediq X.
S.TRANSL: 很多人。
many people

S.FORM: bukuy alang daya peni.
S.TRANSL: 即（馬赫坡）上部落的後方。
It was behind the upper part of the Daya village.

S.FORM: mnoda namu hiya han elu.
S.TRANSL: 你們不是有經過那條路？
Didn't you two pass there before?

S.FORM: pluwe bale laqi na wa pqaya truma na wa muudus na wa paqaya wamin qhuni ini tuting ruru.
S.TRANSL: 他的孩子好可憐，還吊掛在山谷裡而且還活著，吊掛在樹根尚未掉落至（谷底）。
Her child was really pitiful. He was hung in the valley but alive. He was hung from the tree root, and did not fall into valley yet.

S.FORM: wa na odaan qmada hiya laqi na mesa bubu na awi nobing nii.
S.TRANSL: Awi Nobing的媽媽說，Obing Dakis將其孩兒拋入那山谷中。
Awi Nobing's mother said that Obing Dakis was going to throw the child into the deep valley.

S.FORM: tngoo suku a snolan na ma.
S.TRANSL: Suku他們是這麼說的，Obing Dakis與Suku部落的族人一起逃難（Awi Nobing的媽媽是Suku部落的人）。
The Suku people told me that Obing Dakis and the Suku people ran away together (Awi Nobing's mother is from Suku village).

S.FORM: tloong so waga mona rudo ciida ini rengo ani kingan peni.
S.TRANSL: 當時莫那魯道就坐在那裡，他一句話也沒說。
At that time, Mona Rudo was just sitting over there, saying nothing.

S.FORM: ado heya pusu ani kingan ini rengo.
S.TRANSL: 因他是（霧社事件的）關鍵性人物。
It is because he was the key figure of this event.

S.FORM: wada nannichi hari bobona.
S.TRANSL: 大概幾天? 之後...
About how many days? After that...

S.FORM: kesa kesatu nii we sense uri.
S.TRANSL: (在部落的)日本警察也是（兼任）教師嗎?
Did the Japanese police officers also do the teacher's job?

S.FORM: kiya tikuh sense nami ma.
S.TRANSL: 連警察都可以當我們的老師。
Even the police officers could be our teachers.

S.FORM: um uka sense ciida han.
S.TRANSL: 那時候沒有老師。
There were no (real) teachers at that time.

S.FORM: umin rasang msense han na.
S.TRANSL: 最初的老師是Umin Rasang對嗎？
Our first teacher was Umin Rasang, is that right?

S.FORM: uxe heya wa uxe heya.
S.TRANSL: 不是，不是。
No, it was not him.

S.FORM: bobona heya.
S.TRANSL: 之後才是他(Umin Rasang)當老師。
It was later when he began to teach us.

S.FORM: hani su mnusa ini tble ini tbleyaq tmgesa.
S.TRANSL: 妳剛去（上學校）時（老師）不好好（認真）教嗎？
When you went to school, did the teacher not teach you with all his effort?

S.FORM: ini bleyaq tmgesa.
S.TRANSL: 沒好好（認真）教。
No, he did not teach us with his full effort.

S.FORM: um ma ani naq ini usa muuyas uri ini huwa.
S.TRANSL: 就是不上學也沒關係。
It did not matter if you did not go to school.

S.FORM: um mbserux naq sense miyan kiya nii.
S.TRANSL: 我們那位老師懶於教學。
That teacher was lazy at teaching.

S.FORM: um ini tgesa.
S.TRANSL: （他）不教。
He simply did not teach us.

S.FORM: ma kelaun su ngayan ho ma hmuwa di.
S.TRANSL: 妳知道他的名字嗎？接著又如何呢？
Do you still remember his name? Then, what happened?

S.FORM: um ani kingan kanagana ini mu kelai betaq nii na.
S.TRANSL: 到這時候我仍然連一個片假名都不會。
By that time I was going to graduate, I had learned no kana letters at all.

S.FORM: ini su kelai un ma kazaera sang da.
S.TRANSL: 接下來由Kazae Ra 先生（來教我們）。
Then, Mr. Kazae Ra came to teach us.

S.FORM: um um um heya mbeyax riyung malu riyung tgesa na da.
S.TRANSL: 他就非常用心的教而且教的很好。
He was devoted and taught us very well.

S.FORM: o sense sense bale wa.
S.TRANSL: 喔，那麼他是正式的老師了嗎？
So, was he a qualified teacher?

S.FORM: um peeyah kiya wada ku mkela katagana nanaq.
S.TRANSL: 從那時我就會了，僅片假名而已。
From that time on, I learned katakana and only katakana.

S.FORM: um um um piya nami mosa mahung ma yaku ma bakan walis waga ma awe sisuk um.
S.TRANSL: 我們去老師家的有Mahung、我、Bakan Walis以及Awe Sisuk。
Those who went the teacher's house were Mahung, I, Bakan Walis and Awe Sisuk.

S.FORM: to miyan mqedin sepac miyan tgesaan na.
S.TRANSL: 去老師家的我們四位都是女生。
The four of us who went over to his home were girls.

S.FORM: um keeman keeman tgesaan miyan na splawa nami na hii.
S.TRANSL: 每天晚上教我們，叫我們過去。
He taught us every night. He called us to go there.

S.FORM: ma m diyan han we musa hbaro kii han.
S.TRANSL: 去，（白天時）很多（學生）。
During the daytime, there were many students.

S.FORM: theyaq namu han.
S.TRANSL: 白天時.. 妳們在玩嗎（不用讀書）？
During the daytime, did you play?

S.FORM: ma s o ma mbiya keeman de splawa namu na.
S.TRANSL: 哦~到了晚上老師再叫妳們（去上課）。
So, the teacher asked you all to go there at night.

S.FORM: e ma miyan so pusi so yami tgesaan miyan na keeman.
S.TRANSL: 我們就像是去補習，老師晚上教我們。
We were like going to a cram school. The teacher taught us at night.

S.FORM: o ye namu kelaan malu namu hari tunux peni kii splawa namu na keeman dung.
S.TRANSL: 喔，也許妳們... 妳們應該屬於較聰慧的，所以晚上又叫妳們（到老師家上課）。
Perhaps, you are all smarter than others, so you all were asked to go there.

S.FORM: uxe.
S.TRANSL: 沒有。
No.

S.FORM: splawa miyan uka asi ba mmelux tngesaan kesacu wa.
S.TRANSL: 被叫去... 那(一開始都不教的)警察教的一點效果也沒有。
We were asked to go there because the last police officer's teaching did not work.

S.FORM: ani kingan hnyegan katagana ini ku kela.
S.TRANSL: 連一棵片假名（的符號）我都不會。
I knew no kana letters at all.

S.FORM: ima namu mkela hari muuyas sepac namu.
S.TRANSL: 妳們四個當中哪一位的成績比較好？
Among the four of you, which one did better?

S.FORM: bakan mkela hari.
S.TRANSL: Bakan 的成績比較好。
Bakan did better.

S.FORM: katagana nanaq a kingan ba klegan kingan ba nii.
S.TRANSL: 僅片假名而已，僅那一種，只有一種。
It was katakana, only one of the writing systems, and only one kind.

S.FORM: um um wino naq ba kiya ka ini mu shungii.
S.TRANSL: 僅這（片假名）而已，只有這部分我沒有忘記的。
Only this part did I not forget.

S.FORM: uka tmgesa isu di.
S.TRANSL: 就再也沒有其他的老師教妳片假名了嗎？
There was no other teacher to teach you katakana, right?

S.FORM: uka da kiya nanaq muuyas kiya di.
S.TRANSL: 沒有了，就僅那時唸（學）的。
There was no teacher. I only learned that at that time.

S.FORM: kiya um ke peeyah su naq ciida mkela um betaq saya di.
S.TRANSL: 妳是從那時就會（片假名）至今嗎？
You have known katakana since then?

S.FORM: o um na.
S.TRANSL: 嗯。
Yes.

S.FORM: ptgesa miyan kari di ma matis sluhe miyan matis tikuh.
S.TRANSL: 接著我們學習會話以及練習寫字。
Later, we learned conversational skills and practiced writing.

S.FORM: um um um ma ma ani ani naq siida han na matis a matis a tokitama naq kiya han um.
S.TRANSL: 即使是在那時候，寫字是寫字... 但那是偶而為之。
Even at that time, I did some writing, but it was not daily.

S.FORM: ma mangan ku tensyo nii da mntena miyan so icinense da.
S.TRANSL: 信仰天主教後我們又像一年級一樣。
Since I converted to Christianity, we had been like the first grade again.

S.FORM: kiya um ana ani anu knkana patis a qitaun miyan kiya di matis nami klaali.
S.TRANSL: 我們所看的書就非常多，而且我們每天寫字。
We read many books, and we wrote every day.

S.FORM: kika wada su wada ku a wada kiya hayai ku riyung matis uri di.
S.TRANSL: 也讓我在書寫上快得多。
This made me write very fast.

S.FORM: D:wada mdudun mkela.
S.TRANSL: 妳因而... 逐漸進步。
So, you gradually made progress.

S.FORM: um iya han um bale wa.
S.TRANSL: 嗯，等一下。真的。
Wait a second. Yeah, that's right.

S.FORM: laqi gako da kana laqi rseno osaun na.
S.TRANSL: 學校的所有學生都去那裡。
All the students at school went there.

S.FORM: pdooie so a smmalu halung qhuni di peni um.
S.TRANSL: 帶領所有的男學生去練習操作木製槍枝。
He brought all the boys to practice manipulating wooden guns.

S.FORM: osaun na pcaping hiya di.
S.TRANSL: 教他們（類似）操兵的演練。
He taught them (a kind of) military drills.

S.FORM: um ma ini na baka mnuuyas kogako mbeyax heya di.
S.TRANSL: （校際）(軍事訓練)競賽時他們贏了公學校（的學生）。
At for the military competition, they outstripped the students of public school.

S.FORM: o sense nii.
S.TRANSL: 喔，這位老師嗎？
Oh, is that the same teacher?

S.FORM: un sense nii.
S.TRANSL: 是的，就是這位老師。
Yes, it was that teacher.

S.FORM: ma sen seyto mhebu nadisan na.
S.TRANSL: 他所帶領的是馬赫坡（蕃童教育所）的學生？
So, he was in charge of students in Mhebu?

S.FORM: un um a seyto mhebu adisun na pisay a paran kogako paran han paru kii han.
S.TRANSL: 是，他帶領馬赫坡（蕃童教育所）的學生到Baran（霧社）比賽。Baran的學校屬公學校，是大型的學校。
Yes, he brought students of Mhebu to Paran for the competition. The Paran school was one of the public schools, a large school.

S.FORM: um um paru ma yama ka yami han um um kesaun na pisay heya di na ma inu mbeyax heya m.
S.TRANSL: 我們深山裡的小學校卻贏得勝利。
However, our small school in the deep mountain defeated them.

S.FORM: malu riyung tngesaan na to rseno laqi tgesaan na mhebu.
S.TRANSL: 他教的很好。他在馬赫坡教的都是男學生。
He taught us really well. The students he taught in Mhebu were all boys.

S.FORM: um pdooian na halung du ma osaun na pcaping di ma.
S.TRANSL: 他讓學生練習拿著木槍，帶領學生操練。
He let students practice holding wooden guns and instructed them in the drills.

S.FORM: wada sipaqun seediq ita di.
S.TRANSL: 喔，被起義的族人所殺害嗎？
I see. Was he killed by those who arose against the Japanese?

S.FORM: un wada sipaqun di.
S.TRANSL: 是，被殺害了。
Yes, he was killed.

S.FORM: um hawan.
S.TRANSL: 嗯，真是遺憾。
Um, how pitious it was.

S.FORM: keeman rih so pusi.
S.TRANSL: 晚上，猶如補習似的。
It was held at night and it was like having extra lessons.

S.FORM: diyan diyan we.
S.TRANSL: 那麼白天呢？
How about the daytime?

S.FORM: diyan we so tnka so naq ttgesa tnkaxa kiya.
S.TRANSL: 白天時就如一般地上整天課。
During the daytime, we had classes throughout the day.

S.FORM: tnkaxa ki m.
S.TRANSL: 那是整天上課嗎？
Did you have classes for the whole day?

S.FORM: ncuin miyan meepah mhuma sama ma m kmaro miyan phumaan sama.
S.TRANSL: 有時候我們會工作（學習農務），如種菜及整理菜園。
Sometimes, we worked in the field, such as planting vegetables and grooming the vegetable gardens.

S.FORM: um ma o ncuiun miyan na tmgesa ma musa miyan meepah.
S.TRANSL: 有時候教我們（上課）有時工作（學習農務）。
Sometimes we learned things, and sometimes we worked.

S.FORM: tngesa.
S.TRANSL: 喔，教課。
Oh, he taught you.

S.FORM: um so kiya.
S.TRANSL: 就是那樣。
It was just like that.

S.FORM: ma tgesaun namu na so m maanu di muuyas uyas peni uyas.
S.TRANSL: 是，有教妳們，唱歌嗎？
Yeah, did he teach you to sing songs?

S.FORM: m tmgesa uri ma.
S.TRANSL: 他也有教歌。
He taught songs too.

S.FORM: uka riyung uyas cbeyo ini keegu uyas ini so hndure.
S.TRANSL: 以前歌謠不多，歌謠很少，不像現在（很多歌曲）。
There were not many songs in the past, not like today.

S.FORM: ma i mkela namu uyas maanu di maanu kimigayagayo.
S.TRANSL: 妳們會唱kimigayo(日本國歌)嗎？
Could you sing kimigayo (the Japanese anthem)?

S.FORM: un gayo kiya spuuyas mgrebu mgrebu.
S.TRANSL: 會，那（kimigayo）是每天早晨要唱的。
I can, I sang it every morning.

S.FORM: kiya um chang guoge daha.
S.TRANSL: 那是他們（日本）的國歌。
That's their anthem.

S.FORM: um ma so mrai naq so mesa sanmincuyi di peni so kiya.
S.TRANSL: 就如（你們現在）唱三民主義，是一樣的。
That song is like the anthem of Taiwan, R.O.C. now.

S.FORM: um um ma eniqan enpicu.
S.TRANSL: （妳們）有鉛筆嗎？
Did you have pencils?

S.FORM: eniqan.
S.TRANSL: 有。
We did.

S.FORM: heya mnege kana kii.
S.TRANSL: 鉛筆是他們(老師)提供的嗎？
Did they provide you with pencils?

S.FORM: un heya naq mnege kana biqan miyan na patis uri.
S.TRANSL: 是，那些是他們提供的而且也給我們書本。
Yes, we had pencils and books from them.

S.FORM: patis ckuwe we ckuwe kantang.
S.TRANSL: 桌子呢？簡易的桌子？
How about the desk? Was it a simple desk?

S.FORM: ni kantang.
S.TRANSL: 是，有簡易的桌子。
Yes, we had simple desks.

S.FORM: eniqan ka kokubang kahaya ini maanu spatis nami ka.
S.TRANSL: 有這樣大小的黑板, 我們書寫的工具是XXX。
The blackboard was like this size. What we wrote with was...

S.FORM: ini dayang ani tikuh kesaun miyan matis.
S.TRANSL: 寫在黑板的字完全不清楚。
The words on the blackboard were far from clear.

S.FORM: um uka uka patis kii saya dori so hari qreti wa so hari qreti.
S.TRANSL: 現在已沒有那種筆了？（那種筆）像小枝條。
Do you not have the kind of pen now? It is something like a small tree branch?.

S.FORM: um um um so hari nii qitaan ma spatis wa biciq ba m dmayang.
S.TRANSL: 看起來像這個樣子，寫起來它的劃痕（所寫得的字跡）滿淡的。
It looked like this, and the pencial trace was quite light.

S.FORM: um sekibang kesaun nami sekibang knahani.
S.TRANSL: 我們稱它為sekibang (粉筆)。
We called it "sekibang".

S.FORM: kii patisun nami.
S.TRANSL: 我們就是用它來寫字的。
We used that for writing.

S.FORM: um.
S.TRANSL: 嗯。
Um.
